public_engagement_notes: This is a good tree to illustrate some of the complexities of AVTs. It clearly has a story. My interpretation is that the original maiden oak split and fell and one limb survived to become a phoenix tree. Meantime, a birch or perhaps multiple birches grew on the root plate out of browsing reach. This was followed by rowans and then situation and hemlock from the plantation. Now, maybe over 100 years since the original oak fell, there is rowan seeding into the holes in the crook of the birch, which itself has become  a veteran tree.
